name: SimplyBook.me Booking & Scheduling API
what_it_does: Online booking and appointment scheduling platform with REST API — enabling service businesses (salons, clinics, gyms, consultants) to manage bookings, services, providers, and clients with payment integration and marketplace listings.
use_cases: Creating and managing client bookings for service businesses programmatically
Querying available slots for a service/provider to automate scheduling agents
Syncing booking data with CRM systems via REST API
Building custom booking widgets that create bookings via SimplyBook API
Managing service catalog, pricing, and provider availability via API
not_for: Meeting scheduling between peers (use Cal.com or Calendly)
Restaurant reservation systems with complex seating management
Enterprise resource scheduling without service-business focus
best_when: A service business (salon, clinic, gym) needs online booking with API access for custom integration, client management, and payment processing.
avoid_when: You need peer-to-peer meeting scheduling or complex resource management beyond service appointments.

auth.notes: API key from SimplyBook account. Two-step auth: get user token using API key and company login. Admin token vs. client token for different permission levels. Token expires — must refresh periodically.

pricing.paid_tiers: Basic from $9.9/month; Standard from $29.9; Premium from $49.9; Premium Plus from $82.5
pricing.notes: Feature-based pricing tiers. Good for small to medium service businesses. Strong international support with 32 languages. Payment integration with Stripe, PayPal. Marketplace listing option.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per plan; not prominently documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JSON-RPC and REST API with GraphQL option. Two-step token auth adds complexity. Webhook support for booking events. API documentation comprehensive but complex auth flow adds friction.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Booking creation creates new bookings — no deduplication. Availability queries are idempotent reads. Booking modifications are idempotent for same values.
known_agent_gotchas: Two-step auth: get user token first, then use for API calls — token expires
JSON-RPC and REST endpoints co-exist — pick one approach and be consistent
Feature availability depends on plan — API access to some features requires higher tier
Timezone handling critical — bookings must specify correct timezone for slot availability

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Token auth with expiry. GDPR compliant. EU and US data residency. Two-level auth (admin vs client tokens) for access control. Token expiry reduces long-term credential exposure.
